review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for diagnostic questions on long-term temporal modeling with RNNs or LSTMs and the recurrence pattern used in recurrent convolutional networks.

question_bank.question: Why combine CNN features with an RNN or LSTM for long videos?
choices: CNNs can encode local frame or clip information, while the recurrent model aggregates longer-range temporal structure
RNNs make all video computation fully parallel
CNNs cannot produce features for clips at all
LSTMs remove the need for any training data
question_bank.explanation: Short clips may capture only local motion. Feeding a sequence of CNN features into an RNN or LSTM helps the model connect events that are far apart in time.

question_bank.question: In a recurrent convolutional network, what are the two main inputs used to compute a feature map at a given layer and time step?
choices: The same layer from the previous time step and the previous layer from the current time step
Only the current RGB frame and the final class score
Only the previous hidden state and no spatial features
The optical flow map and the audio spectrogram
question_bank.explanation: Recurrent convolution organizes computation across both depth and time. Each feature map depends on temporal history from its own layer and new information arriving from the lower layer at the current step.
